title: Butonica racemosa
intro: Butonica racemosa, also known as Butonica, is a plant species used in traditional medicine, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and its ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as wounds, gastrointestinal disorders, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Butonica racemosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Butonica racemosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
